macro_segments.title: Three Places Where Halacha Contradicts the Simple Sense of Scripture

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Rabbi Yochanan in the Name of Rabbi Yishmael
First Case: Kissui HaDam—Dirt Can Be Anything That Grows Seeds
Second Case: Cutting the Hair of a Nazir—Any Tool Works, Not Just a Razor
Third Case: Writing a Get—Any Surface Works, Not Just Parchment

Why These Three? Rashi's Answer: No Derasha Available in Scripture

macro_segments.title: Scholarly Debate on Whether Chazal Start with Tradition or Derive from Verses

macro_segments.title: Why Ashes Are Not on Rabbi Yishmael's List Despite Beit Hillel's Inclusion

micro_segments.title: The Question: If Ashes Work for Sotah, Why Not on the List?
Answer One: The List Is Abbreviated; Multiple Items Were Left Out
Answer Two: Shaving Metzora Like a Gourd Adds Rather Than Contradicts
The Metzora Case: Two Different Hermeneutical Approaches (Klal U'Prat vs. Ribui U'Miut)
Answer Three: The Cucumber Shaving Reflects Rabbi Akiva's Hermeneutics, Not Halacha Okevet

macro_segments.title: The Blood-Water Mixture for Metzora: How Much Water Can Be Added?

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yishmael's Interpretation: Separate Mention Means Blood Must Remain Recognizable
How Much Water: Limited to One Revi'it So Blood Remains Predominant

macro_segments.title: Rav Yirmiya's Challenge: Does the Revi'it Measure Work for All Birds?

micro_segments.title: The Question: Large Birds Overwhelm Water; Small Birds Are Overwhelmed by Water

The Point: Within the Normal Range, Revi'it Works; Don't Be Pedantic

macro_segments.title: Order of Mixing: Must Dirt Go Into Water, or Can Water Go onto Dirt?

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: If Dirt Is Added First, It Is Invalid

What Red Heifer Teaches Sotah: Both Require Dirt Visible on Water
What Sotah Teaches Red Heifer: Order Can Be Reversed

macro_segments.title: Red Heifer Pasuk Analysis: Two Verses on the Order of Ashes and Water

micro_segments.title: First Verse: 'Put Upon It' (Alav) Suggests Ashes First
Second Verse: 'To the Vessel' (El Keli) Suggests Water First
Rabbi Shimon's Reconciliation: You Can Do It Either Way

macro_segments.title: The Rabbis' Position: Water Must Go First in Both Sotah and Red Heifer

micro_segments.title: The Principle: The Potent Ingredient (Machshir) Goes On Top
Reinterpreting 'Put Upon': After Water Is Present, Add Dirt and Mix
Supporting Examples: Sotah, Red Heifer, and Bird's Blood All Start with Water
Why Water First Matters More: The Non-Water Element Is the Special Component
